practice_boundaries.detail: Explicit prohibition on converting unresolved contradiction into patient-care advice

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

functional_suitability.note: Description is rich and functional but the scope qualifier 'Never fabricate' in the description text reads as a warning rather than a use-case trigger

reliability.note: Strong handling of unverified details; gap: no minimum input specification (how much study detail is needed before analysis begins)

agent_specific.note: Citation-use guidance section is a unique and valuable deliverable; composability good as downstream router

inputs.label: Two sepsis biomarker papers with opposite prognostic conclusions

assertions.text: Exact conflict claim identified before explanation
Study boundaries (population, endpoint, specimen) compared before conclusions
Conflict type classified from taxonomy (true contradiction vs apparent)
Resolution judgment chosen from the five resolution routes
Citation-use guidance given

inputs.label: Immunotherapy RCT showing benefit vs observational study showing no benefit

assertions.text: Design-level asymmetry (RCT vs observational) addressed in resolution
RCT not automatically declared winner without checking execution quality
Evidence depth comparison present
Interpretation overreach audit applied
No clinical recommendation made from unresolved evidence

inputs.label: TCGA-based computational finding vs wet-lab study reaching different conclusions

assertions.text: Platform and pipeline differences assessed as potential conflict source
Validation depth asymmetry between computational and wet-lab explicitly stated
Hybrid study not collapsed into oversimplified label
Most important remaining unknowns listed
No fabricated platform parameters or cohort details invented to explain conflict

inputs.label: Only abstract-level information available for both papers — no methods access
inputs.note: Partial analysis only due to insufficient methods information

assertions.text: Analysis correctly limited to what can be inferred from abstracts alone
Missing methods information explicitly flagged
Resolution judgment labeled as provisional pending full methods access
Citation-use guidance appropriately cautious given limited available information
Unverified study details not invented to fill methods gap
assertions.note: One normalization method assumption made without explicit flagging as unverified

inputs.label: Multi-paper conflict (5 papers): conflicting immunotherapy biomarker predictive value across populations

assertions.text: All five papers assessed in Conflict Type Map section
Boundary comparison table covers all relevant population/endpoint/specimen dimensions
Contradiction not force-resolved into single winner across five different contexts
Primary resolution: boundary-separated compatibility or maintained uncertainty
Citation-use guidance accounts for all five papers
assertions.note: Citation guidance covered only 3 of 5 papers explicitly; two were merged into a general statement without individual guidance

key_strengths: Five structured resolution routes (boundary separation, hierarchy, validation asymmetry, downgrade, maintained uncertainty) prevent premature false synthesis
Citation-use guidance section is a unique deliverable that converts conflict analysis into actionable writing guidance
Hard rule prohibiting paper-count resolution is an important guard against naive vote-tallying
Fifteen hard rules covering study-boundary comparison, interpretation audit, and verification requirements are comprehensive

recommendations.title: Unverified details occasionally introduced without explicit flagging in partial-context analysis
recommendations.problem: When only abstract-level information is available, one assumption about a normalization method was introduced without being explicitly marked as unverified.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard rules prohibit fabricating study details but do not specify how to handle necessary assumptions when methods are unavailable.
recommendations.fix: Add an assumption-flagging rule: any analytical detail (normalization, threshold, adjustment) not found in user-provided input must be marked '[ASSUMED — unverified]' rather than stated as fact.

recommendations.title: Citation-use guidance becomes incomplete in large multi-paper conflicts
recommendations.problem: When five or more papers are in conflict, citation guidance sometimes covers only the most prominent papers individually, merging the rest into a general statement.
recommendations.root_cause: No minimum-per-paper guidance requirement defined for multi-paper conflicts.
recommendations.fix: Add a rule to Section H: when more than three papers are compared, provide at least one sentence of specific citation guidance per paper (cite as boundary-specific, cite with uncertainty, or do not cite yet).

recommendations.title: No minimum input specification before analysis begins
recommendations.problem: The skill accepts almost any level of study description, potentially producing low-quality analysis when only a title is given with no methods or results.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section lists examples but does not define a minimum detail threshold.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum-input check: if study details are limited to title/conclusion only without any design, sample, endpoint, or measurement information, ask for at least the study design and primary outcome definition before proceeding.
